info.title: AP | Approvals
info.description:
BILL is a cloud-based cash flow management system for an organization's Accounts Payable (AP) and Accounts Receivable (AR) automation.
 * **AP**: Automate workflows to pay vendors bills at scale for the goods and services they provide. Pay vendors with a wide range of payment methods. Generate vendor credits, bill credits, and payment disbursement reports.
 * **AR**: Automate workflows to get paid by customers at scale for the goods and services you provide. Generate and send invoices on a schedule, generate customer and invoice credits, and seamlessly charge authorized customers.
 * **Organization**: Manage your bank accounts (for sending and receiving payments), users (with user roles), and sync with your choice of accounting software.

Integrate with the BILL API to send and receive payments for your business. See [Support Center](https://help.bill.com/hc/en-us) for resources about using the BILL web app.

post.description:
Create a new approval policy.

In your request, set `type` as the approval policy type. See the parameters for the complete list of types. In addition, you can set policies for a payment amount threshold (`amountThreshold`) and minimum number of approvers (`minNumApprovers`).

### What is an approval policy?
You can create approval policies at the organization-level for approving bills or vendor credits. 

When you create a policy, you can set rules, such as payment amount threshold, mandatory approvers, and minimum number of approvers. For example, you can set the payment amount threshold (`amountThreshold`) as `$1000` and minimum number of approvers (`minNumApprovers`) as `2`.

When you enter bills or vendor credits in the BILL web app, the Smart Data Entry (SDE) feature is used to auto-assign approvers that have been assigned to earlier bills or vendor credits. These approvers are also auto-assigned to new bills or vendor credits created with the API.

When you apply an approval policy to a bill or vendor credit object, you can designate specific approvers (including the sequence of approvals) with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

post.description:
Get a list of all approval policies.

In the response, you get details about whether each policy is for a specific vendor or for a bill amount threshold. In addition, mandatory approvers or approver groups are listed for each policy.

post.description:
Add an approval policy approver to an approval policy.

### Who is an approval policy approver?
You can assign approvers to an approval policy that requires one or more mandatory approvers. In addition, you can set multiple approvers to a policy with `/v2/Bulk/Crud/Create/ApprovalPolicyApprover.json`. 

When you apply an approval policy to a bill or vendor credit object, you can designate specific approvers (including the sequence of approvals) with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.

**Note**: Replacing an approver in an approval policy is a two-step process.
1. Delete the existing approver from the policy with `/v2/Crud/Delete/ApprovalPolicyApprover.json`.
2. Add the new approver(s) with `/v2/Crud/Create/ApprovalPolicyApprover.json`.

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

post.description:
Read a bill approver object.

In your request, set `id` as the bill approver ID.

### Who is a bill approver?
You can assign approvers to a bill that requires one or more mandatory approvers. When you apply an approval policy to a bill object, you can designate specific approvers (including the sequence of approvals) with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.

**Note**: To replace an approver assigned to a bill or to change the sequence of approvals, remove all approvers assigned to the vendor credit (with `/v2/ClearApprovers.json`) and then designate specific approvers (including the sequence of approvals) with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

post.description:
Read a vendor credit approver object.

### Who is a vendor credit approver?
You can assign approvers to a vendor credit that requires one or more mandatory approvers. When you apply an approval policy to a vendor credit object, you can designate specific approvers (including the sequence of approvals) with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.

**Note**: To replace an approver assigned to a vendor credit or to change the sequence of approvals, remove all approvers assigned to the vendor credit (with `/v2/ClearApprovers.json`) and then designate specific approvers (including the sequence of approvals) with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

### Who is a vendor credit approver?
You can assign approvers to a vendor credit that requires one or more mandatory approvers. When you apply an approval policy to a vendor credit object, you can designate specific approvers (including the sequence of approvals) with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.

**Note**: To replace an approver assigned to a vendor credit or to change the sequence of approvals, remove all approvers assigned to the vendor credit (with `/v2/ClearApprovers.json`) and then designate specific approvers (including the sequence of approvals) with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.

List approvers assigned to a bill or vendor credit.

In your request, set `objectId` as the bill or vendor credit ID. In the response, the list of approvers is retrieved in the `sortOrder` of approvals.

post.summary: List bill & vendor credits assigned to a user
post.description:
List bill credits and vendor credits assigned to a user.

See [Search operations with lists](doc:search-ops-with-lists) to learn about pagination, sorting, and filtering.

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

post.summary: Set approvers for a bill or vendor credit
post.description:
Set specific approvers for your bill or vendor credit.

In your request, set `objectId` as the bill or vendor credit ID and `approvers` as an array of user IDs set to approve the listed bill or vendor credit. The order in which you add users in the array is the sequence of approvals required. For example, `["{user_id1}", "{user_id2}", "{user_id3}"]`. You can list users with `/v2/List/Users.json`.

The added users must have the required approver permissions. Set approver permissions with `/v2/Crud/Create/User.json` or `/v2/Crud/Update/User.json`. In your request, set `profileId` for the Administrator, Accountant, or Approver permissions (retrieved with `/v2/List/Profile.json`).

When you set approvers with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`, bill approver or vendor credit approver IDs are created and the first approver in the sequence receives an email for approval.

You can retrieve approvers assigned to the bill or vendor credit with `/v2/ListApprovers.json`.

### Set approver use cases
You can use `/v2/SetApprovers.json` for a range of use cases.
* When your organization wants to assign chosen approvers to a bill or vendor credit before payment
* When an approval policy (created with `/v2/Crud/Create/ApprovalPolicy.json`) requires an approval policy approver (created with `/v2/Crud/Create/ApprovalPolicyApprover.json`).

### Approver actions and endpoint behavior changes 
When an approver acts on (approves or denies) a bill or vendor credit, setting approvers with `/v2/SetApprovers.json` results in specific changes.
* If an approver acts on a bill or vendor credit and then you set the approver again with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`, the approver is ignored in the new sequence of approval.
* If an approver has not acted on a bill or vendor credit and if you set a new approver with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`, the new approver replaces the existing approver.
* If none of the approvers have acted on a bill or vendor credit and if you set new approvers with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`, the new sequence replaces the current sequence.

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

post.summary: Clear approvers for a bill or vendor credit
post.description:
Clear all approvers for your bill or vendor credit.

After the approvers are cleared, you can assign approvers with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.

**Warning**: When you clear approvers with `/v2/ClearApprovers.json`, all approvers assigned to the bill or vendor credit are removed. This is regardless of whether the bill or vendor credit is approved, denied, or yet to be reviewed.

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

post.summary: Approve a bill or vendor credit
post.description:
Approve a bill or vendor credit.

Only users with approver permissions can be assigned to the bill or vendor credit for approval. If the bill or vendor credit requires multiple approvals, the next approver in the sequence receives an email for approval after a previous approval.

You can assign approvers for different use cases.
* Setting approvers for a bill or vendor credit with `/v2/SetApprovers.json` 
* Setting approvers for an approval policy with `/v2/Crud/Create/ApprovalPolicyApprover.json` or `/v2/Crud/Update/ApprovalPolicyApprover.json`

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

post.summary: Deny the approval of a bill or vendor credit
post.description:
Deny the approval of a bill or vendor credit.

When the approval of the bill or vendor credit is denied, the approval process stops. After the reason for denying the approval is resolved in the bill or vendor credit, clear all approvers with `/v2/ClearApprovers.json` and then set approvers with `/v2/SetApprovers.json`.

devKey.description: Developer key generated with your BILL developer account

type.description:
Approval policy type.
* `0`: Bill
* `1`: Vendor credit

amountThreshold.description: Payment amount threshold for the policy

bankAccountId.description: BILL-generated ID of the bank account. The value begins with `bac`. Set this value when you set `type` as `1`.

usersId.description: BILL-generated user ID of a mandatory policy approver. The value begins with `006`.

approverNotSameAsPayer.description: Set as `true` if the approver and payer cannot be the same user

approverCondition.description:
Approving conditions. You can set this value for policy rules.
* `0`: Priority AND
* `1`: AND
* `2`: OR

isActive.description:
Object visibility status. Inactive objects are hidden by default in the BILL web app.
* `1`: Active
* `2`: Inactive

response_status.description:
Response status.
* `0`: Success response
* `1`: Error response. Only `error_code` and `error_message` is returned in `response_data`.

response_message.description:
Response status.
* `Success`: Success response
* `Error`: Error response. Only `error_code` and `error_message` is returned in `response_data`.

id.description: BILL-generated ID of the approval policy. The value begins with `app`.

name.description: Approval policy name automatically generated by BILL

type.description:
Approval policy type.
* `0`: Bill
* `1`: Vendor credit

amountThreshold.description: Payment amount threshold for the policy

bankAccountId.description: BILL-generated ID of the bank account. The value begins with `bac`. Set this value when you set `type` as `1`.

usersId.description: BILL-generated user ID of a mandatory policy approver. The value begins with `006`.

approverNotSameAsPayer.description: Set as `true` if the approver and payer cannot be the same user

approverCondition.description:
Approving conditions. You can set this value for policy rules.
* `0`: Priority AND
* `1`: AND
* `2`: OR

isActive.description:
Object visibility status. Inactive objects are hidden by default in the BILL web app.
* `1`: Active
* `2`: Inactive

createdById.description: BILL-generated user ID of the user that created the approval policy. The value begins with `006`.

response_status.description:
Response status.
* `0`: Success response
* `1`: Error response. Only `error_code` and `error_message` is returned in `response_data`.

response_message.description:
Response status.
* `Success`: Success response
* `Error`: Error response. Only `error_code` and `error_message` is returned in `response_data`.

id.description: BILL-generated ID of the approval policy. The value begins with `app`.

field.description: Field name. See the response body documentation to learn about fields that you can filter with. Filterable fields are marked with `You can filter with this field.`

op.description:
Operator symbol.
* `=`: Equals
* `<`: Less than
* `>`: Greater than
* `!=`: Not equal
* `<=`: Less than or equal to
* `>=`: Greater than or equal to
* `in`: Value in list
* `nin`: Value not in list

asc.description:
Sort order.
* `true`: Ascending order
* `false`: Descending order

nested.description: Set as `true` to include additional nested data in the response

response_status.description:
Response status.
* `0`: Success response
* `1`: Error response. Only `error_code` and `error_message` is returned in `response_data`.

response_message.description:
Response status.
* `Success`: Success response
* `Error`: Error response. Only `error_code` and `error_message` is returned in `response_data`.

id.description: BILL-generated ID of the approval policy. The value begins with `app`. You can filter with this field.

name.description: Approval policy name automatically generated by BILL

type.description:
Approval policy type. You can filter with this field.
* `0`: Bill
* `1`: Vendor credit

amountThreshold.description: Payment amount threshold for the policy. You can filter with this field.

bankAccountId.description: BILL-generated ID of the bank account. The value begins with `bac`. Set this value when you set `type` as `1`. You can filter with this field.

usersId.description: BILL-generated user ID of a mandatory policy approver. The value begins with `006`. You can filter with this field.

approverNotSameAsPayer.description: Set as `true` if the approver and payer cannot be the same user. You can filter with this field.

approverCondition.description:
Approving conditions. You can set this value for policy rules. You can filter with this field.
* `0`: Priority AND
* `1`: AND
* `2`: OR

isActive.description:
Object visibility status. Inactive objects are hidden by default in the BILL web app. You can filter with this field.
* `1`: Active
* `2`: Inactive

createdTime.description: Created date and time. You can filter with this field.

createdById.description: BILL-generated user ID of the user that created the approval policy. The value begins with `006`. You can filter with this field.

updatedTime.description: Updated data and time. You can filter with this field.

response_status.description:
Response status.
* `0`: Success response
* `1`: Error response. Only `error_code` and `error_message` is returned in `response_data`.

response_message.description:
Response status.

userType.description: User type. Set as `User` for an individual user. Set as `ApproverGroup` for an approver group.
